title: Neisosperma apoensis
taxonomy: Neisosperma apoensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Olacaceae. It is native to the Philippines and was first described by the botanist F.R. Fosberg in 1977. The classification of Neisosperma apoensis places it within the kingdom Plantae, in the clade Angiosperms, and further into the class Magnoliopsida. This species is part of the order Santalales and is known to be a member of the family Olacaceae. Neisosperma apoensis is considered a rare and endemic plant, found only in the specific region of the Philippines.
uses: Neisosperma apoensis is a plant species native to the Philippines, and research is being conducted on its potential medicinal properties. The plant contains alkaloids and other bioactive compounds that may have therapeutic benefits. However, more studies are needed to fully understand the medicinal uses of Neisosperma apoensis.
